note:
{
  "experiment_overview": "The experiment involves the fabrication of nanopillar-based Split Ring Resonators (SRRs) for terahertz metamaterials using displacement current-mediated resonances. The goal is to compare the Q-factor and frequency shift between film-based SRRs and nanopillar-based SRRs.",
  "procedures_observed": [
    "Evaporate Cr and Cu seed layer",
    "Electroplate Au thin film",
    "Deposit ALD Al2O3 coating",
    "Fabricate Split Ring Resonators with 10 nm Al2O3 gaps"
  ],
  "materials_and_subjects": [
    "High-resistivity Si substrate",
    "Au",
    "Cr",
    "Cu",
    "ALD Al2O3",
    "Split Ring Resonators"
  ],
  "tools_and_setup": [
    "Vacuum chamber",
    "Plasma etching machine",
    "SEM (Scanning Electron Microscope)",
    "Optical microscope"
  ],
  "quantitative_observations": [
    "Q-factor comparison between film-based and nanopillar-based SRRs",
    "Frequency shift measurements",
    "SEM images showing nanoscale structures"
  ],
  "key_transitions": [
    "From deposition of seed layers to electroplating of Au",
    "Transition from vacuum deposition to plasma etching",
    "Shift from optical imaging to SEM analysis"
  ],
  "outcomes_or_indicators": [
    "Comparison graphs showing Q-factor and frequency shift",
    "SEM images demonstrating nanoscale structures",
    "Optical images of fabricated SRRs"
  ],
  "anything_unusual_or_notable": [
    "Use of displacement current for resonance generation",
    "Comparison of different SRR designs",
    "Detailed visualization of nanoscale structures"
  ]
}
